name: Puget Sound Institute of Science
description: Graduate-focused institute for marine and environmental science in the South Sound. Home of the Coastal Ecology Lab.

name: Cascade Range University
description: Public university serving the South Puget Sound region. Strong programs in biology and environmental science.

name: Salish Sea Research Cooperative
description: Collaborative research on Salish Sea nearshore ecosystems, water quality, and species monitoring.

name: Seattle Aquarium
description: Nonprofit aquarium and conservation organization on Seattle's waterfront dedicated to ocean health.

name: Padilla Bay National Estuarine Research Reserve
description: Estuarine research reserve protecting eelgrass habitat and supporting coastal science and education.

name: Washington Department of Natural Resources
description: Manages Washington's public lands and aquatic resources, including the Aquatic Resources Division.

name: Washington Sea Grant
description: University of Washington program supporting marine research, outreach, and coastal community resilience.

name: Point Defiance Zoo and Aquarium
description: Tacoma zoo and aquarium focused on Pacific Northwest species and marine conservation.

name: NOAA Northwest Fisheries Science Center
description: Federal marine science center studying fisheries, salmon recovery, and ecosystem health on the West Coast.

name: Puget Sound Restoration Fund
description: Nonprofit restoring native shellfish, kelp, and nearshore habitat across Puget Sound.

name: The Whale Trail
description: Network of sites along the Salish Sea coast for viewing and protecting orcas and other marine mammals.

name: Oregon Institute of Marine Biology
description: University of Oregon coastal marine laboratory for research and graduate training.

name: Friday Harbor Laboratories
description: University of Washington marine field station in the San Juan Islands for teaching and research.

name: Hakai Institute
description: Long-term ecological research on coastal margins of the northeast Pacific.

name: Surfrider Foundation
description: Grassroots nonprofit dedicated to the protection of oceans, waves, and beaches.

name: MaST Center Aquarium
description: Highline College marine science and technology center on the shores of Puget Sound.

name: Western Society of Naturalists
description: Scientific society for marine and intertidal ecology research along the Pacific coast.

name: Ecological Society of America
location: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description: Professional society advancing ecological science and its application.

name: Olympic Coast National Marine Sanctuary
description: NOAA marine sanctuary protecting 3,188 square miles of Washington's outer coast.

name: Northwest Straits Marine Conservation Initiative
description: Regional network of marine resources committees protecting the northern Salish Sea.

name: Tacoma Public Library
description: Public library system serving the city of Tacoma and surrounding communities.

name: Billion Oyster Project
location: New York, New York, United States
description: Nonprofit restoring oyster reefs and engaging students in marine ecosystem restoration.

name: Long Live the Kings
description: Nonprofit restoring wild salmon and steelhead and supporting sustainable fishing in the Salish Sea.

name: Marine Conservation Institute
description: Science-based nonprofit working to secure protection for the ocean's most important places.
